description_long: This object is a modern airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a pair of swept-back wings, each featuring a subtle dihedral angle. The aircraft is shown in an exploded view, revealing internal components such as avionics, engine modules, and landing gear assemblies. The tail section is equipped with a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating a single pilot. The exterior is primarily finished in a smooth, metallic gray with blue-tinted accents on the wings and cockpit area. Various mechanical and electronic parts are visible, indicating a complex internal structure typical of a functional urban aircraft.
description: A sleek, modern airplane with swept-back wings, a conventional tail, and an enclosed forward cockpit, shown in an exploded view with visible internal components.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit canopy, and the leading edges of the wings with blue-tinted accents.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the fuselage, the swept-back wings, and the separated internal modules and tail assembly.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the open internal structure of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the tail and wings separated, exposing the internal arrangement of components and modules.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept-back wings and a slight dihedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear, all shown in separated positions.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single-seat.
